etymology.description: semantic 食(食) (shí, 'food') + phonetic 余(餘) (yú, 'I') -> remains of food -> 'excess, surplus'
etymology.interpretation: The character 餘(餘) evolved to represent the concept of 'excess' or 'surplus' through a phono-semantic compound. It combines the semantic component 食(食) (shí, 'food'), representing food or consumption, with the phonetic component 余(餘) (yú, 'I'), to indicate leftovers or remains of food. This directly suggests an aspect of having more than what is needed, thus an 'excess' or 'surplus'.
etymology.simplification: The traditional character 餘 was simplified to 余 by removing the semantic component 食(食) (shí, 'food'), leaving only the phonetic component.

examples.chinese: 余(餘)愿以诗书为乐(願以詩書為樂)。
examples.pinyin: Yú yuàn yǐ shīshū wéi lè.
examples.english: I wish to take pleasure in poetry and books.

examples.chinese: 余(餘)在此地(在此地)，心安吾归(心安吾歸)。
examples.pinyin: Yú zài cǐdì, xīn ān wú guī.
examples.english: I am here, at peace and at home.

examples.chinese: 余(餘)莫负此生(莫負此生)。
examples.english: I should not waste this life.
